dialogos.texto_en: That exhibition earlier was interesting, wasn't it?
Yeah!\nThere were many different animals\nin the Yanbaru Forest.
But, what does Yanbaru mean?
I don't know either.\nIt has a jungle-like ring to it,\nmaybe it's related?
Wrong.\nDon't judge by how the word sounds.
Ah, big bro.
Yanbaru is written in kanji as 'mountain field' in Japanese,\nreferring to a place in the northern part of Okinawa with large\nmountains and forests. Many species are exclusive to Yanbaru.
Ah, could it be that Yanbaru rail is also from Yanbaru?
Yes, that's right.\nThe rail is a type of bird, and it was called the\nYanbaru rail because it lives in the Yanbaru Forest.
I see.
We won't be going during this trip, but I've heard\nthat one of the ways to enjoy Okinawa is to\nappreciate the unique nature of the Yanbaru forest.
Seemingly, you can see and experience the natural landscapes that's untouched by humankind.
Wow! That sounds fun in itself!\nLike an adventure!
There are many things you can do in Okinawa,\naside from swimming in the sea.
Judging from how much you guys already know,\nhave you both visited the Yanbaru Forest exhibition?
Yeah! They had separate exhibits showcasing different forests,\none for daytime and one for nighttime, and they were displayed\naccording to the lifestyle of the animals.
They had an exhibit of the Yanbaru rail\nwalking along the water during the day.\nIt was so cute!
I couldn't find it,\nso Raiha-chan showed me where it was.
Really? Given its fame, the Yanbaru rail ought to have been on show somewhere that stood out.
It's displayed according to its ecology to educate people on how to spot them.
Ah, it's because they hide in locations that are difficult for predators to find.
I see, the exhibition is well thought out.\nInteresting.
Yeah, it was intriguing to learn how they live in their environment.
By the way, I really thought the Iriomote cat was cute.
The contrast between its fierce demeanor and its cute face was so charming.
Ah%p-2;――%p; yeah!\nThe Iriomote cat was super cute too!
How about you, big bro? Which one did you think was cuter?
I don't see them the same way as you guys do.\nThe Yanbaru rail is simply just a bird,\nand the Iriomote cat is simply just a cat.
I don't really get the whole finding animals\ncute or not in the first place.
Big bro...
Ummm%p-2;――%p; so in your eyes are they all the same?\nLike, all living creatures are the exact same?
Oh, by the way, Uesugi-san.\nYou're not with Miku?
Y-yeah.\nWe just haven't run into each other yet.
The Yanbaru rail!
You chose almost instantly, there?!\nYou didn't even hesitate!
The reason is clear!\nIf Raiha thinks is cute, then it's cute!
Ahaha...\nThat logic is so Uesugi-san coded.
Oh, by the way, Uesugi-san.\nYou're not with Miku?
Y-yeah.\nWe just haven't run into each other yet.
Both of them are endangered species, right?
Oh, yeah.\nThere was an explanation\nthat it's an endangered species.
I see, then maybe we shouldn't be calling them cute.
No, that's not it. It's important to be interested\nin any way, shape or form. In the end,\nit might lead to saving them from extinction.
If you knew that the animal you thought was cute\nwas an endangered species, you would want to do\nsomething to help, right?
Yes, I just thought of that too.
Me too!
There's nothing we can do to help right now,\nbut it's important that we keep that fact in mind.
It's important to educate ourselves and become aware of these facts at places like this, right?
Ohhh.\nBig bro, you're like a teacher.
Yeah, you should aim to become a teacher\nwith Itsuki.
Nah, I've learned from hard experience\nnot to do that.\nIt was SO tough being your tutor, guys.
Though you say that, you don't really seem\nto be entirely uninterested, do you?
Big bro, the teacher, eh.
Oh, by the way, Uesugi-san.\nYou're not with Miku?
Y-yeah.\nWe just haven't run into each other yet.
Actually, I have something I want to ask you\nabout Miku, Yotsuba.
What about her?
I thought you might know the cause\nof why she's acting strangely.
I was asked about it earlier,\nbut do you have any idea?
Ah, that's what you meant.
Hmm%p-2;――%p; I also don't know at all.\nThat's why I asked you, Uesugi-san.
I see...
I was also wondering about it, too.\nMiku-san has been acting a little strange, huh?
Big bro, did you do something to upset her?
No, I really have no idea what's going on...
